TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy_Albert_II, title = Albert_II, year = 2025, month = May, day = 5, note = {In Wikipedia [Sakizaya]}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albert_II} }

S.FORM: == Albert II (Monako) == u siwkay niza 3 bulad 14 demiad, Albert II, u misingkiway. 1958 a mihca nalecuhan. u tabakiay a tapang nu kanatal (congtung) nu Monaco ayza sa ci Albert II, micakat a demiad sa i 2005 a mihca 4 bulad 6 demiad. nalecuhan ciniza i Monako Kuowang a luma'. u Monako a Kuowang . u Monako tabakiay a kakelidan. u ngangan niza u ngangan nu Hulam: ťėŅÁąĺŤ≤ĚšļĆšłĖ u ngangan nu ś≥ēŤ™ě: Albert Alexandre Louis Pierre Grimaldi kakuniza u sulit nu Hilam atu Amilika Albert II is the reigning monarch of the Principality of Monaco and head of the princely house of Grimaldi. He is the son of Prince Rainier III and the American actress Grace Kelly. Prince Albert's sisters are Caroline, Princess of Hanover, and Princess St√©phanie. In July 2011, Prince Albert married Charlene Wittstock. Prince Albert II is one of the wealthiest royals in the world, with assets valued at more than $1 billion, which include land in Monaco and France. While Prince Albert's real estate does not include the Prince's Palace of Monaco, it does include holdings in the Soci√©t√© des bains de mer de Monaco, which operates Monaco's casino and other entertainment properties in the principality.
S.PHON: == ar~…ĺb…ôrt ii (monako) == u siwkaj niza * bur~…ĺad ** d…ômiad, ar~…ĺb…ôrt ii, u misiŇčkiwaj. **** a mińß ¶a nar~…ĺ…ô ¶uńßan. u tabakiaj a tapaŇč nu kanatar~…ĺ ( ¶oŇčtuŇč) nu mona ¶o ajza sa ¶i ar~…ĺb…ôrt ii, mi ¶akat a d…ômiad sa i **** a mińß ¶a * bur~…ĺad * d…ômiad. nar~…ĺ…ô ¶uńßan ¶iniza i monako kuowaŇč a r~…ĺuma Ē~ °. u monako a kuowaŇč . u monako tabakiaj a kak…ôr~…ĺidan. u ŇčaŇčan niza u ŇčaŇčan nu ńßur~…ĺam: ***** u ŇčaŇčan nu **: ar~…ĺb…ôrt ar~…ĺ…ô*andr…ô r~…ĺouis pi…ôrr…ô grimar~…ĺdi kakuniza u sur~…ĺit nu ńßir~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika ar~…ĺb…ôrt ii is tńß…ô r…ôigniŇč monar ¶ńß of tńß…ô prin ¶ipar~…ĺitj of mona ¶o and ńß…ôad of tńß…ô prin ¶…ôr~…ĺj ńßous…ô of grimar~…ĺdi. ńß…ô is tńß…ô son of prin ¶…ô *aini…ôr iii and tńß…ô am…ôri ¶an a ¶tr…ôss gra ¶…ô k…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺj. prin ¶…ô ar~…ĺb…ôrt Ē~ °s sist…ôrs ar…ô ¶aror~…ĺin…ô, prin ¶…ôss of ńßano*…ôr, and prin ¶…ôss st*pńßani…ô. in *ur~…ĺj ****, prin ¶…ô ar~…ĺb…ôrt marri…ôd ¶ńßarr~…ĺ…ôn…ô wittsto ¶k. prin ¶…ô ar~…ĺb…ôrt ii is on…ô of tńß…ô w…ôar~…ĺtńßi…ôst rojar~…ĺs in tńß…ô worr~…ĺd, witńß ass…ôts *ar~…ĺu…ôd at mor…ô tńßan $* bir~…ĺr~…ĺion, wńßi ¶ńß in ¶r~…ĺud…ô r~…ĺand in mona ¶o and fran ¶…ô. wńßir~…ĺ…ô prin ¶…ô ar~…ĺb…ôrt Ē~ °s r…ôar~…ĺ …ôstat…ô do…ôs not in ¶r~…ĺud…ô tńß…ô prin ¶…ô Ē~ °s par~…ĺa ¶…ô of mona ¶o, it do…ôs in ¶r~…ĺud…ô ńßor~…ĺdiŇčs in tńß…ô so ¶i*t* d…ôs bains d…ô m…ôr d…ô mona ¶o, wńßi ¶ńß op…ôrat…ôs mona ¶o Ē~ °s ¶asino and otńß…ôr …ônt…ôrtainm…ônt prop…ôrti…ôs in tńß…ô prin ¶ipar~…ĺitj.
S.FORM: == Albert II (Monako) == u siwkay niza 3 bulad 14 demiad, Albert II, u misingkiway. 1958 a mihca nalecuhan. u tabakiay a tapang nu kanatal (congtung) nu Monaco ayza sa ci Albert II, micakat a demiad sa i 2005 a mihca 4 bulad 6 demiad. nalecuhan ciniza i Monako Kuowang a luma'. u Monako a Kuowang . u Monako tabakiay a kakelidan. u ngangan niza u ngangan nu Hulam: ťėŅÁąĺŤ≤ĚšļĆšłĖ u ngangan nu ś≥ēŤ™ě: Albert Alexandre Louis Pierre Grimaldi kakuniza u sulit nu Hilam atu Amilika Albert II is the reigning monarch of the Principality of Monaco and head of the princely house of Grimaldi. He is the son of Prince Rainier III and the American actress Grace Kelly. Prince Albert's sisters are Caroline, Princess of Hanover, and Princess St√©phanie. In July 2011, Prince Albert married Charlene Wittstock. Prince Albert II is one of the wealthiest royals in the world, with assets valued at more than $1 billion, which include land in Monaco and France. While Prince Albert's real estate does not include the Prince's Palace of Monaco, it does include holdings in the Soci√©t√© des bains de mer de Monaco, which operates Monaco's casino and other entertainment properties in the principality.
